game_name: Landstalker: The Treasures of King Nole

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Jypta Ruins - introductory dungeon with rolling boulders and moving platforms
Vertical navigation puzzles in early dungeons
Ogres, skeletons, standard fantasy creatures in early areas
Progressive complexity through dungeons and overworld
Escalates with game progression; Mir the wizard is notable mid-game boss
Platform and movement-based traps increase in complexity
Purchasable in towns; progression tied to exploration and gold collection

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Throughout dungeons and purchasable in towns
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Heart containers increase maximum health; items restore health mid-adventure
Variety increases with dungeon progression; some enemies must be defeated to advance
Optional challenges with rewards (increased attack power, shortcut warps)
Required for plot advancement; optional areas for resource gathering
Reward for NPC quest completion; reduces overworld traversal time

typical_relief_triggers: Completing dungeon section and facing boss
Earning side quest rewards and shortcuts
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering trap sequences (rolling boulders, moving platforms)
Increased enemy density in mid-to-late game areas
Locked door blocking progression (key hunt tension)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-release through dungeon-town-dungeon structure; early game emphasizes puzzle-over-combat difficulty; mid-to-late game escalates both enemy threat and environmental complexity; boss encounters serve as climactic tension peaks followed by plot exposition and relief

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side paths in dungeons and overworld areas lead to optional treasures, enemy encounters for gold, and NPC side quests; clearly distinct from critical path
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Exploration of non-obvious directions and hidden areas yields collectibles and optional upgrades; encouraged but not mandatory

difficulty_curve.early_game: Jypta Ruins introduces basic platforming and simple combat; rolling boulders and moving platforms teach rhythm without severe punishment; combat encounters avoidable or simple one-on-one duels
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Massan, Gumi, Ryuma dungeons increase enemy variety (different creature types require different tactics); multi-room dungeons with puzzle chains; Mir the Wizard boss represents significant power spike; resource management becomes important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dungeons feature complex trap sequences combining platform navigation with combat; boss encounters require pattern recognition and tactical dodging; enemy density increases; environmental hazards more deadly
notable_spikes: Mir the Wizard (single combat, significant mid-game progression gate)
Duke Mercator revelation (narrative shock leading to dungeon escape sequence)
Post-Zak encounter dungeons (combined difficulty spike from Zak abduction plot point)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game alternates between dungeon sections (challenge, puzzles, combat, boss) and town visits (NPC interaction, healing, shopping, plot exposition). Creates macro-level rhythm of tension and relief across entire adventure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Jypta Ruins → Kalva town → Massan dungeon → Massan village → Gumi dungeon → Gumi village

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons combine platforming puzzles (block pushing, lever switching) with combat encounters; some require solving puzzle to access combat area, others require defeating enemies to proceed to next puzzle chamber
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wizard's Tower (Mir dungeon) combines crypt platforming with tower boss combat

notable_level_patterns.description: Locked doors appear throughout dungeons; finding keys requires backtracking, exploration, or defeating optional enemies. Creates non-linear internal structure within linear overall progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple dungeons use this; Ryuma Thieves' Cave uses kidnapped NPC rescue as key-equivalent plot gate

notable_level_patterns.description: Major boss defeats (Mir) lead to plot revelations rather than immediate progression. Creates narrative weight and justifies boss difficulty spike
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mir the Wizard reveals Duke's conspiracy after defeat; serves as major plot pivot

notable_level_patterns.description: NPCs in towns offer side challenges separate from main dungeon path; completion grants mechanical advantages (attack power, shortcuts) without blocking critical path
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Massan bear-tribe rescue (Fara) integrated into main plot but completion is mechanical gating, not narrative gating
